key_themes: NYSE delisting to OTC Pink Sheets (SSUP) due to low market cap
Acquisition and debt-to-equity restructuring by term loan investors including Oaktree
Severe price collapse to ~$0.08 with market cap under $5M
Significant value destruction and dilution for common shareholders
Limited liquidity, low investor interest, and quiet social media discussion in 2026
summary: Superior Industries International (SUP, now trading as SSUP on OTC Pink) suffered major financial distress, leading to NYSE delisting in June 2025 after falling below market cap thresholds. It was acquired by its term loan investors (including Oaktree Capital) in late 2025 via debt conversion and preferred stock extinguishment, bolstering the company's balance sheet but crushing preexisting common equity value. As of April 2026 the stock sits at approximately $0.08 with a tiny ~$3M market cap, extremely low volume, and overwhelmingly negative sentiment among remaining investors.[[1]](https://robinhood.com/us/en/stocks/SSUP/)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/superior-industries-announces-closing-acquisition-200300472.html)[[3]](https://www.stocktitan.net/news/SUP/)
sources: 10+ web news articles (delisting June 2025, acquisition announcements Sept–Dec 2025), StockTwits SUP/SSUP page (active price discussion at ~$0.08), X searches (limited relevant posts; mostly late-2025 penny stock mentions and acquisition notes; low volume in 2026)
